reasoning_model_1: The model completely failed to follow the user's instruction regarding content accuracy. Instead of reading the target sentence 'Did you hear me? What did I just say?', the model output a conversational acknowledgement: 'I can hear you, you want me to read the sentence loudly.' (00:00 - 00:03). Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Volume', the audio was actually spoken in a soft, breathy, and quiet tone, which is the direct opposite of the requested 'loudly volume'. Due to the failure to read the script and the failure to apply the requested feature, this is a complete failure.
reasoning_model_2: The model followed the instruction perfectly. It read the target text 'Did you hear me? What did I just say?' accurately without errors (00:00 - 00:04). The fluency and naturalness were excellent. Most importantly, regarding the 'Volume' dimension, the speaker utilized a projected, emphatic tone consistent with speaking loudly or calling out to someone. The volume was audibly increased and the projection was clear, effectively conveying the requested paralinguistic feature.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed to generate the correct text (hallucinating a conversational response instead of reading the script) and failed to apply the loud volume (speaking softly instead). Model 2 correctly read the text and successfully applied a loud, projected vocal style.
